name: Golden Hour Rule
description: The Golden Hour Rule is a tactic used in crisis communications to respond to a crisis within the first hour of its occurrence. This helps to mitigate the crisis and prevent it from escalating.
when_to_apply: In the initial stages of a crisis, when the situation is still developing
example: A company experiences a data breach and immediately issues a statement to the public, acknowledging the breach and outlining the steps being taken to address it
anti_pattern: Waiting too long to respond to a crisis, allowing it to escalate and causing more damage

name: Fact-Checking
description: Fact-checking is a tactic used to verify the accuracy of information. This helps to prevent the spread of false information and maintain credibility.
when_to_apply: Before publishing any information, especially in situations where the information may be sensitive or controversial
example: A journalist fact-checks a piece of information before publishing it, to ensure that it is accurate and reliable
anti_pattern: Publishing information without verifying its accuracy, which can lead to the spread of false information
